title: Hollow Earth: The Descent, Vol. 1
tonic_progression: Bbm (Aeolian, the threshold from Telos's open F dominant) -> Fm (Dorian, propulsion) -> Cm (Dorian) -> Gm (Aeolian) -> Dm (Phrygian flavor) -> Am (Phrygian) -> Em (Phrygian-dominant menace, megafauna apex) -> Bm (Aeolian) -> F#m (Dorian, the long fall) -> C#m (Aeolian) -> G#m / Abm (Phrygian, deepest/darkest point) -> Ebm (Aeolian, the inner-expanse handoff, open Bb dominant leaning UP toward Agartha's inner sun)
narrative_arc: Twelve tracks carry the listener from the polar threshold Telos left open down through the lithosphere into the awe of the inner expanse, where the saga's next chapter waits. This is the saga's DESCENT album: where Telos warmed upward into light, Hollow Earth drives DOWNWARD into the deep, darker and more propulsive, and unlike Telos's lowest-and-slowest BPM band this record is built to MOVE. Tracks 1-3 are the polar opening and the first plunge: the expedition stands at the rim of the polar opening Telos's floor became, the cold aurora-lit vortex of the entrance turning overhead while the DESCENT-CRYSTAL motif (Telos's crystal arpeggio, now darkened to a minor-add9 shape in the low register, its contour falling) rings the threshold (1, Bb minor, the only drumless track here, the held breath before the dive); the propulsion ignites and the descent proper begins down a vast spiralling shaft of ice giving way to ancient stone (2, F minor Dorian, the album's drive arrives); and the walls open into the first great hollow gallery where the temperature climbs and the air begins to glow faintly from below (3, C minor Dorian). Tracks 4-7 are the deepening through prehistoric strata: the shaft levels into a cavern of luminous fungal forests and the first stirrings of something vast moving in the dark (4, G minor); the expedition crosses an underground sea on black water under a stone sky, megafauna shadows passing beneath the hull (5, D minor, Phrygian color creeping in); the propulsion bottoms into a bone-floored hunting ground where the apex predators of the inner world wake (6, A minor Phrygian, the album darkening hard); and track 7 is the MEGAFAUNA APEX — the album's peak BPM and its most menacing harmony, a full Phrygian-dominant chase as a colossal saurian thing gives pursuit through a forest of stone trees (7, E minor Phrygian-dominant, BPM ceiling 118, the propulsive zenith). Tracks 8-11 are the long fall past the predators toward the bottom of the world: the chase breaks and the floor drops away again into a deeper shaft (8, B minor); the descent lengthens through a region of slow geological grandeur, magma-lit galleries the size of cities (9, F# minor Dorian); the air thins and cools and the dark deepens toward the lowest point the expedition will reach (10, C# minor); and the DEEPEST, DARKEST POINT of the album, a black abyssal gulf where the propulsion goes quiet and the DESCENT-CRYSTAL motif rings alone, falling, the bottom of the descent (11, G# / Ab minor Phrygian, the album's near-floor). Track 12 is the inner-expanse handoff: the abyss does not close so much as OPEN OUTWARD. The walls fall away entirely and the listener emerges, awed, into a vast inner space where — impossibly far below and ahead — a faint warm light begins (the INNER-SUN VOICING, withheld for the whole album, returns at last as a distant rumor on the horizon). The harmony settles into E-flat minor and the closing cadence resolves onto an open Bb major-minor dominant that leans UPWARD and OUTWARD toward that light rather than home — a threshold opening into the inner world. A listener moving from this final bar into Agartha, Vol. 1 should feel the deep give way to the inner sun and the saga turn from descent toward arrival.

recurring_motifs.motif: DESCENT-CRYSTAL (saga leitmotif #1, transposed and darkened from Telos's CRYSTAL ARPEGGIO) — the same crystalline bell/glass arpeggio figure, but transposed into Hollow Earth's home key and DARKENED: a minor-add9 shape (1-5-b9... no, root-fifth-flat-ninth is wrong; voiced root-5-b3-9 as a minor-add9, struck in the LOW register one to two octaves below Telos's bright placement, on a darker FM bell + sub-glass layer with a long cavern-reverb tail) and with its CONTOUR FALLING rather than rising. This is the saga's primary recurring figure, carried forward from Telos and bent to the descent — it is Telos's crystal city heard from far below the floor that opened, ringing as the expedition falls past it.
recurring_motifs.transformation: Track 1 introduces the DESCENT-CRYSTAL ringing the threshold — minor-add9, low register, the contour falling, picking up exactly where Telos track 12 fragmented-and-turned-it-downward (the saga seam made audible: the listener should recognize the crystal city's motif, now darker and falling, the moment Hollow Earth begins). Track 6 plays it FRAGMENTED AND DRIVEN — chopped into a propulsive 16th-note ostinato under the predator section, the bell figure weaponised into rhythm as the inner world turns hostile. Track 7 (megafauna apex) plays it CHROMATICALLY DESCENDING through the Phrygian-dominant harmony, the falling contour stretched into a long chromatic plunge mirroring the chase downward. Track 11 (deepest point) plays it ALONE AND FALLING, slow and unaccompanied over the abyssal pad, the propulsion gone silent — the crystal heard one last time at the bottom of the world, each phrase dropping into the dark. Track 12 turns it OUTWARD: the same pitch-set but the contour opens horizontally rather than falling, the figure widening as the walls fall away into the inner expanse, handing the motif toward Agartha. SAGA NOTE: in Agartha Vol. 1 (album #3) this figure should return at its most RADIANT near the inner sun, transposed up into bright register — the saga's payoff inversion of this darkened descent statement.

recurring_motifs.motif: INNER-SUN VOICING (saga leitmotif #2, WITHHELD across this album until the final handoff) — the wide warm slowly-blooming major chord established in Telos (open fifths + major third + ninth across five octaves, wordless choir pad + warm analog strings, sidechain almost fully off). In Telos it bloomed at the transcendence apex; here it is DELIBERATELY WITHHELD for the entire descent — the listener leaves the inner sun behind at the top and falls through eleven tracks of dark, propulsive deep with no warmth, so that its single distant return at the very end lands as the saga's first promise of arrival.
recurring_motifs.transformation: Heard NOWHERE in tracks 1-11 (its absence IS the descent — the deep is cold and lightless, the warmth left above). Returns ONLY in track 12, FAINT AND DISTANT ON THE HORIZON: as the walls fall away into the inner expanse, the voicing surfaces impossibly far below and ahead, low in the mix, heavily filtered, root rising slowly out of the dark rather than dropping — the first rumor of the inner sun the expedition has descended toward. It does NOT bloom here; it only appears, a promise. SAGA NOTE: this voicing is the harmonic SEED and PAYOFF of Agartha Vol. 1 (album #3) — Agartha should OPEN near this distant statement and RESOLVE INTO it at full bloom as the saga's inner-sun arrival. Hollow Earth's job is to make the listener want it back by withholding it; Agartha's job is to give it.

recurring_motifs.motif: PROPULSION PULSE (album-local drive figure) — a driving low analog-bass-and-kick ostinato, root-octave-fifth eighth-note pattern with a hard-square sub bass and a tight gated kick, the engine of the descent. This is what makes Hollow Earth MOVE where Telos was meditative; it is the propulsion of the dive itself.
recurring_motifs.transformation: Track 2 introduces it as the descent ignites — the album's drive arriving for the first time after the drumless threshold of track 1, a steady purposeful eighth-note plunge. Track 4 deepens it with a syncopated octave-jump as the shaft levels into the first cavern. Track 6 drives it harder and faster under the predator section, the pulse turning anxious. Track 7 (megafauna apex) takes it to its most relentless — the chase tempo, the engine at full strain. Track 9 returns it SLOW AND VAST — the same ostinato stretched out and given enormous reverb as the descent passes through the magma-lit galleries the size of cities, the propulsion now grand rather than urgent. It is deliberately ABSENT from the slow/quiet tracks (1, 11, 12) where the dark and the awe take over.

tracks.title: The Polar Opening

sections.name: intro
sections.instrumentation: cold deep analog pad on Bb alone, no rhythm, a low wind-and-ice noise floor, the DESCENT-CRYSTAL motif ringing the threshold for the first time (minor-add9, low register, contour FALLING) once per 8-bar phrase — picking up exactly where Telos track 12 left it

sections.name: verse
sections.instrumentation: + wordless choir pad entering cold underneath, deep sub bass on the root holding long whole notes, the descent-crystal continuing to fall, distant aurora-shimmer FM tones high above

sections.name: chorus
sections.instrumentation: + a slow descending saw lead tracing the lip of the opening, layered cold atmospheric pads with reverse-reverb wash, still no drums, the sense of standing at the rim of a vast vortex turning overhead

sections.name: bridge
sections.instrumentation: everything thins to pad + the falling descent-crystal + a single sustained choir note, the held breath before the dive

sections.name: outro
sections.instrumentation: pad sustains Bb, the descent-crystal rings and falls one final time, no rhythm has ever entered, the harmony leaning toward F (the dominant) and the dive about to begin

tracks.musicgen_prompt: synthwave 88 BPM B-flat minor, cold deep analog pad, low wind and ice noise floor, a falling low-register crystalline bell arpeggio in a minor-add-nine shape ringing in a vast reverberant space, wordless choir pad entering cold, deep sub bass on long whole notes, distant high aurora-shimmer bell tones, slow descending saw lead, layered cold atmospheric pads with reverse reverb, no drums at all, the held breath at the rim of a vast polar opening into the deep earth, anticipatory and cold and drawn downward, instrumental, no vocals
tracks.cover_prompt: Lost Worlds saga album cover, cold deep-earth palette of indigo teal and aurora green with a faint warm amber glow far below, a lone fur-and-canvas-clad expedition figure standing at the rim of an enormous circular polar opening in a vast ice sheet, a slow aurora-lit vortex of cold light turning in the sky overhead, the shaft descending into geological strata and darkness far below, breath fogging in the cold, threshold anticipatory mood, painterly retro-1980s art, no faces visible (figure seen from behind at the brink), no text, 1024x1024
tracks.key_relationship_to_prior: Picks up the saga seam directly: Telos Vol. 1 closed on an OPEN F major-minor dominant leaning DOWNWARD into Bb minor. Hollow Earth opens in Bb minor — the dominant Telos left open resolves here, but DOWN into the dark rather than home, exactly as Telos's track 12 promised. Same Bb-minor tonal center Telos's descent-handoff settled onto; the saga's floor and the album's threshold are the same pitch.
tracks.tempo_relationship_to_prior: Telos's descent-handoff sat at 82 BPM (near-floor, settling-and-sinking). Hollow Earth opens at 88 — a step UP from where Telos left off, signalling that the propulsive DESCENDING arc Telos handed off to is about to begin. This is the only track here without drums; the propulsion arrives next track.
tracks.composer_notes: OPENER / SAGA SEAM PICKUP. This track must make the listener recognize the saga continuing: the DESCENT-CRYSTAL motif is Telos's crystal arpeggio, now darkened to a low-register minor-add9 with its contour FALLING — picking up exactly where Telos track 12 fragmented-and-turned-it-downward. The home tonic Bb minor resolves the open F dominant Telos left, but down into the dark. This is the album's only drumless track (the threshold breath before the dive) and one of its slowest at 88 — Hollow Earth, unlike Telos, is built to MOVE, so the stillness here exists only to make the propulsion of track 2 land. The INNER-SUN voicing is deliberately ABSENT (it was left receding-upward at the Telos handoff and is withheld until track 12). Do NOT let MusicGen add a bright lead or warm major chord — this is cold, vast, drawn-down. Outro leans toward F (V of Bb) but stays unresolved — the dive begins next track.

tracks.title: Ignition of the Dive

sections.name: intro
sections.instrumentation: cold pad in F Dorian (raised 6th, D natural, lending a forward lean), the PROPULSION PULSE motif igniting for the first time (driving root-octave-fifth eighth-note ostinato on hard-square sub bass + tight gated kick), the album's drive arriving

sections.name: verse
sections.instrumentation: + tight gated reverb drums locking in (kick on 1 and 3, snare on 2 and 4, driving), deep sub bass, a cold arpeggio descending stepwise, sense of plunging down a vast spiralling shaft of ice

sections.name: chorus
sections.instrumentation: + a purposeful cold saw lead on a descending phrase, the Dorian D natural keeping the drive forward-leaning, layered cold pads, hard sidechain pumping with the propulsion, momentum building downward

sections.name: breakdown
sections.instrumentation: drums drop to just the propulsion-pulse ostinato + pad, the listener feels the shaft narrowing, ice giving way to ancient stone

sections.name: chorus2
sections.instrumentation: full drive returns, lead doubled an octave down on a darker FM voice, propulsion relentless

sections.name: outro
sections.instrumentation: filter close on C (V of F), pad sustains, the propulsion pulse drives one more phrase then holds

tracks.musicgen_prompt: synthwave 96 BPM F minor Dorian (raised sixth, D natural lending forward lean), cold analog pad, a driving root-octave-fifth eighth-note bass-and-kick ostinato, tight gated reverb drums with a propulsive backbeat, deep hard-square sub bass, a cold stepwise-descending arpeggio, a purposeful cold descending saw lead, layered cold pads, hard sidechain pumping, sense of plunging down a vast spiralling shaft of ice into ancient stone, propulsive and purposeful and cold, instrumental, no vocals
tracks.cover_prompt: Lost Worlds saga album cover, cold deep-earth palette of indigo teal and ice-blue with a faint warm glow far below, a steep spiralling shaft descending through a vast wall of glacial ice that gives way to dark geological strata deeper down, a small expedition vehicle or figure plunging down the spiral path, motion-blur streaks of cold light, the polar opening shrunk to a bright ring far above, propulsive plunging mood, painterly retro-1980s art, no faces visible, no text, 1024x1024
tracks.key_relationship_to_prior: Down a perfect fifth from Bb minor to F minor (F was the dominant Telos left open and that track 1 leaned toward; rerooting it as the F-minor tonic turns the dominant tension into forward propulsion). The Dorian raised-6th (D natural) gives the minor a forward, driving lean rather than a mournful one — the descent has purpose.
tracks.tempo_relationship_to_prior: +8 BPM — the album's first big jump, deliberately placed here as the PROPULSION PULSE ignites and the dive begins in earnest. Where Telos's increments were tiny and patient, Hollow Earth's are larger and accelerating: this is a driving record.
tracks.composer_notes: FIRST APPEARANCE of the PROPULSION PULSE (album-local drive figure) and the arrival of the album's signature MOVEMENT. After the drumless threshold of track 1, the tight gated kit and the driving eighth-note bass-and-kick ostinato ignite here — this is the engine of the descent. F Dorian (raised 6th) keeps the drive forward-leaning rather than grieving; this is purposeful plunge, not lament. Hard sidechain is appropriate here (unlike Telos's breathing near-off sidechain) — Hollow Earth is the driving sibling. Outro C (V of F) primes C minor of track 3 (C as dominant rerooted into C minor — the descent continues down).

tracks.title: First Hollow Gallery

sections.name: intro
sections.instrumentation: pad in C Dorian (raised 6th, A natural), the propulsion pulse driving, a faint warmer undertone creeping into the cold pad as the temperature climbs, soft gated kick

sections.name: verse
sections.instrumentation: + tight gated reverb drums driving, deep sub bass, a stepwise arpeggio, the gallery walls opening wide, distant low resonance as if the space is enormous

sections.name: chorus
sections.instrumentation: + a driving saw lead on a discovery phrase, the Dorian A natural lending a faint warmth, layered pads with a first hint of amber glow from below, hard sidechain, the air beginning to glow faintly

sections.name: breakdown
sections.instrumentation: drums drop, just the propulsion pulse + pad + a distant glimmer of warmth, the first great hollow space revealed

sections.name: chorus2
sections.instrumentation: full drive returns, lead doubled on FM bell, the gallery vast around the descent

sections.name: outro
sections.instrumentation: filter close on G (V of C), pad sustains, propulsion drives one more phrase

tracks.musicgen_prompt: synthwave 102 BPM C minor Dorian (raised sixth, A natural lending faint warmth), cold analog pad with a faint warmer undertone creeping in, a driving propulsive bass-and-kick ostinato, tight gated reverb drums, deep sub bass, a stepwise arpeggio, a driving discovery saw lead, layered pads with a first hint of amber glow from below, distant enormous low resonance, hard sidechain, sense of a spiralling shaft opening into the first vast hollow gallery deep in the earth where the air begins to glow faintly, driving and vast and discovery, instrumental, no vocals
tracks.cover_prompt: Lost Worlds saga album cover, cold deep-earth palette warming faintly toward amber, the descent shaft opening out into an enormous vaulted hollow gallery of ancient stone deep beneath the surface, the ceiling lost in darkness above, a faint warm amber glow rising from chasms in the gallery floor far below, geological strata banding the vast walls, a tiny expedition figure for scale at a ledge, driving discovery mood, painterly retro-1980s art, no faces visible, no text, 1024x1024
tracks.key_relationship_to_prior: Down a perfect fourth / continuing the descending-fifths logic from Fm to Cm (C was the V that closed track 2, rerooted as the C-minor tonic — the descent moves down through the cycle, each new tonic a step deeper). The Dorian raised-6th (A natural) lets a first faint warmth creep into the cold as the temperature climbs deeper down.
tracks.tempo_relationship_to_prior: +6 BPM — the propulsion keeps accelerating as the descent gathers momentum. Hollow Earth's BPM arc RISES through the first half (toward the megafauna apex) where Telos's stayed low and meditative — this is the saga's driving album.
tracks.composer_notes: The descent's first major space. C Dorian (raised 6th, A natural) lets the FIRST FAINT WARMTH creep into the cold pad — a deliberate harmonic foreshadow that the deep earth, counter-intuitively, GLOWS (the geothermal/inner-sun logic of the saga). This is NOT the inner-sun voicing yet (that's withheld to track 12); just a faint amber undertone. Propulsion pulse continues driving. Outro G (V of C) primes G minor of track 4 (G as dominant rerooted into G minor — the descent continues down through the cycle).

tracks.title: Luminous Forests

sections.name: intro
sections.instrumentation: pad in G Aeolian minor (now naturally minor, the warmth of track 3 turning uneasy), the propulsion pulse deepened with a syncopated octave-jump, soft gated kick, eerie bioluminescent FM glints

sections.name: verse
sections.instrumentation: + tight gated reverb drums driving, deep sub bass with the syncopated octave-jump, an arpeggio of cold-glowing bell tones like fungal light, a first low rumble of something vast moving in the dark

sections.name: chorus
sections.instrumentation: + a driving saw lead on a phrase of alien beauty turning uneasy, layered pads glowing eerie green-violet, hard sidechain, the bioluminescent forest around the descent and the first stirring of life beneath

sections.name: breakdown
sections.instrumentation: drums drop, the syncopated propulsion + eerie bell glints + the low rumble surfacing, something vast stirring

sections.name: chorus2
sections.instrumentation: full drive returns, lead doubled with a darker FM counter-line, the unease growing under the beauty

sections.name: outro
sections.instrumentation: filter close on D (V of G), pad sustains, the low rumble holds into the next track

tracks.musicgen_prompt: synthwave 108 BPM G minor, eerie cold analog pad turning uneasy, a syncopated driving octave-jumping bass-and-kick ostinato, tight gated reverb drums, deep sub bass, an arpeggio of cold-glowing bell tones like fungal bioluminescence, a low rumble of something vast moving in the dark, a driving saw lead of alien beauty turning uneasy, layered pads glowing eerie green and violet, hard sidechain, sense of crossing a vast underground forest of luminous fungi with the first stirring of enormous life beneath, alien-beautiful and uneasy and driving, instrumental, no vocals
tracks.cover_prompt: Lost Worlds saga album cover, cold deep-earth palette with eerie bioluminescent green and violet, a vast underground forest of towering glowing fungal trees and luminescent flora deep within a hollow cavern, cold green-violet light pooling on the cavern floor, a faint warm amber glow still rising far in the distance, the hint of an enormous dark shape moving between the glowing trunks, a small expedition figure dwarfed by the fungal canopy, alien-beautiful uneasy mood, painterly retro-1980s art, no faces visible, no text, 1024x1024
tracks.key_relationship_to_prior: Down a perfect fourth from Cm to Gm (G was the V that closed track 3, rerooted as the G-minor tonic — the descending cycle continues). The mode shifts from Dorian (raised-6th warmth) to natural Aeolian minor here: the faint warmth of the upper galleries turns UNEASY as the first life stirs — the descent begins to darken in earnest.
tracks.tempo_relationship_to_prior: +6 BPM — propulsion still accelerating toward the apex. The syncopated octave-jump deepens the PROPULSION PULSE here, the drive turning more anxious as the inner world reveals it is inhabited.
tracks.composer_notes: The descent's beauty turns uneasy. The PROPULSION PULSE deepens with a syncopated octave-jump (its track-4 transformation). The shift from Dorian to natural Aeolian minor is the harmonic signal that the inner world is INHABITED and the descent is no longer safe — the first low rumble of megafauna surfaces in the breakdown. Eerie bioluminescent bell glints (green-violet) replace the faint amber warmth of track 3. Outro D (V of G) primes D minor of track 5 (D as dominant rerooted into D minor — and Phrygian color begins creeping in).

tracks.title: The Underground Sea

sections.name: intro
sections.instrumentation: pad in D minor with a Phrygian b2 (Eb) darkening the harmony, the propulsion pulse driving low, a watery convolution-reverb tail, soft gated kick, the sound of black water under a stone sky

sections.name: verse
sections.instrumentation: + tight gated reverb drums driving, deep sub bass, the Phrygian b2 (Eb) glinting menacingly, low passing shadows of megafauna under the hull rendered as deep sub-swells

sections.name: chorus
sections.instrumentation: + an ominous driving saw lead, the Phrygian b2 lending a dark exotic menace, layered dark pads, hard sidechain, the sense of crossing a vast black underground sea with enormous shapes passing beneath

sections.name: bridge
sections.instrumentation: drums drop, just the low propulsion + watery reverb + a single deep sub-swell as a megafauna shadow passes directly beneath, ominous stillness on the dark water

sections.name: chorus2
sections.instrumentation: full drive returns, lead doubled low on FM bell, the menace and the vastness growing

sections.name: outro
sections.instrumentation: filter close on A (V of D), pad sustains, a final deep sub-swell as something vast submerges

tracks.musicgen_prompt: synthwave 112 BPM D minor with a Phrygian flat-second (E-flat darkening the harmony), dark analog pad, a driving low propulsive bass-and-kick ostinato, tight gated reverb drums, deep sub bass, watery convolution reverb, deep sub-bass swells like enormous shapes passing beneath black water, an ominous driving saw lead with dark exotic Phrygian menace, layered dark pads, hard sidechain, sense of crossing a vast black underground sea under a stone sky with megafauna shadows passing beneath the hull, vast-dark and ominous and deep, instrumental, no vocals
tracks.cover_prompt: Lost Worlds saga album cover, cold deep-earth palette of black water and dark teal with faint amber glow on the far horizon, a vast still black underground sea stretching under an enormous stone-vaulted sky deep within the hollow earth, a small expedition raft or vessel crossing the dark water, the huge dark silhouette of a sea-saurian passing just beneath the surface leaving a wake, faint phosphorescence trailing in the water, a distant warm glow on the cavern horizon, ominous vast-dark mood, painterly retro-1980s art, no faces visible, no text, 1024x1024
tracks.key_relationship_to_prior: Down a perfect fourth from Gm to Dm (D was the V that closed track 4, rerooted as the D-minor tonic — the descending cycle continues). PHRYGIAN COLOR (the b2, Eb) creeps in here for the first time — the album's harmony begins its turn toward the darkest modes as the descent reaches the inhabited deep. (Note: D minor is also the saga's Telos home tonic, here re-cast dark and Phrygian — the same pitch the saga warmed in Telos is now submerged and menacing, a deliberate saga echo.)
tracks.tempo_relationship_to_prior: +4 BPM — still climbing toward the megafauna apex, the propulsion driving steadily. The increments tighten near the peak.
tracks.composer_notes: BRIDGE into the predator arc. PHRYGIAN COLOR (b2 = Eb) is introduced here, beginning the album's harmonic darkening toward the megafauna apex. Megafauna are rendered as DEEP SUB-SWELLS passing beneath — felt more than heard, the menace implied. SAGA ECHO: this track's D-minor tonic is the same pitch as Telos's home key, here re-cast dark and Phrygian — the saga's warm center submerged and made menacing, a deliberate tonal callback for an end-to-end listener. Outro A (V of D) primes A minor of track 6 (A as dominant rerooted into A-minor Phrygian — the predators wake).

tracks.title: Bone-Floor Hunting Ground

sections.name: intro
sections.instrumentation: dark pad in A Phrygian (b2 = Bb prominent), the DESCENT-CRYSTAL motif now FRAGMENTED AND DRIVEN (chopped into a propulsive 16th-note ostinato), the propulsion pulse harder and faster, gated kick driving

sections.name: verse
sections.instrumentation: + hard gated reverb drums, deep sub bass, the Phrygian b2 (Bb) lending menace, the chopped descent-crystal ostinato weaponised into rhythm, bone-rattle percussive FM hits, the apex predators waking

sections.name: chorus
sections.instrumentation: + a menacing driving saw lead, the Phrygian darkness at full, layered dark pads, hard sidechain, the chopped crystal ostinato driving underneath, predatory tension

sections.name: breakdown
sections.instrumentation: drums drop to just the chopped descent-crystal ostinato + a low predator growl rendered as detuned sub, the hunting ground revealed, bones underfoot

sections.name: chorus2
sections.instrumentation: hard drive returns, lead doubled with a snarling detuned counter-line, the menace at full

sections.name: outro
sections.instrumentation: filter close on E (V of A), pad sustains, the chopped crystal ostinato drives into the chase of track 7

tracks.musicgen_prompt: synthwave 116 BPM A minor Phrygian (flat-second, B-flat prominent), dark analog pad, a chopped propulsive 16th-note crystalline-bell ostinato, a hard fast propulsive bass-and-kick drive, hard gated reverb drums, deep sub bass, bone-rattle percussive hits, a low detuned predator growl in the sub, a menacing driving saw lead with full Phrygian darkness, layered dark pads, hard sidechain, sense of crossing a bone-floored hunting ground deep in the earth where apex predators wake, menacing and predatory and driving hard, instrumental, no vocals
tracks.cover_prompt: Lost Worlds saga album cover, cold deep-earth palette of dark slate and blood-amber, a vast cavern floor littered with the colossal bleached bones of prehistoric beasts deep within the hollow earth, dark stone walls rising into shadow, a faint sinister amber glow from deep fissures, the glinting eyes and partial silhouette of an enormous waking predator in the shadows at the cavern edge, a small expedition figure frozen in the foreground, menacing predatory mood, painterly retro-1980s art, no faces visible, no text, 1024x1024
tracks.key_relationship_to_prior: Down a perfect fourth from Dm to Am (A was the V that closed track 5, rerooted as the A-minor tonic — the descending cycle continues). Full PHRYGIAN mode now (b2 = Bb prominent) — the album darkening hard as the predator ground is reached, the harmony at its most menacing before the Phrygian-dominant apex.
tracks.tempo_relationship_to_prior: +4 BPM — one step below the apex, the propulsion near its hardest. The drive turns anxious and fast as the predators wake.
tracks.composer_notes: The predator ground. The DESCENT-CRYSTAL motif returns FRAGMENTED AND DRIVEN — chopped into a propulsive 16th-note ostinato, the saga's crystal figure weaponised into menacing rhythm (its track-6 transformation: the same crystal city motif, now a predator's pulse in the dark). Full Phrygian mode (b2 = Bb) is the album's darkest harmony so far, setting up the Phrygian-DOMINANT apex of track 7. Hard, fast, tense — but still 116 BPM, within band, never frantic-EDM (the audience is coding). Outro E (V of A) is reread as the dominant leading into E-Phrygian-dominant for the apex chase — a chromatic intensification, not a key drop.

tracks.title: The Great Pursuit

sections.name: intro
sections.instrumentation: dark pad in E Phrygian-dominant (b2 = F, raised 3rd = G#, the exotic menacing tension), the PROPULSION PULSE at its most relentless (chase tempo), reverse cymbal swell, hard gated kick driving

sections.name: verse
sections.instrumentation: + hard gated reverb drums at full drive, deep sub bass, the DESCENT-CRYSTAL motif now CHROMATICALLY DESCENDING through the Phrygian-dominant harmony (the falling contour stretched into a long chromatic plunge), the colossal saurian thing giving chase

sections.name: pre-chorus
sections.instrumentation: build: rising riser, snare rolls, filter opening, the chase tightening

sections.name: chorus
sections.instrumentation: the album's most relentless moment: a terrifying driving saw lead in E Phrygian-dominant (the b2/raised-3rd tension screaming), the chromatically-descending crystal beneath, full hard kit, hard sidechain, the great pursuit through a forest of stone trees

sections.name: verse2
sections.instrumentation: lead steps back, the chromatic-descent crystal + propulsion carry the chase, the predator gaining

sections.name: break
sections.instrumentation: drums drop to a half-time stomp, a single colossal detuned roar in the sub, the predator at its closest — the album's terror zenith held for one breath

sections.name: chorus2
sections.instrumentation: full hard return, the most relentless mix on the album, the chase at full strain

tracks.tempo_relationship_to_prior: +2 BPM — the album's BPM ceiling at 118 (within the 80-118 validation range, deliberately maxed here for the chase). tracks.composer_notes: PEAK / MEGAFAUNA APEX. This is the album's terror-and-speed zenith — the inverse of Telos's serene light-apex. The mode is PHRYGIAN-DOMINANT (b2 = F over E, raised 3rd = G#) — the most exotic, menacing harmony in the catalog, the chromatic intensification of the predator ground. The DESCENT-CRYSTAL motif is CHROMATICALLY DESCENDING here (its falling contour stretched into a long plunge through the harmony, mirroring the chase downward). The half-time stomp break at bar 41 (a single colossal detuned roar, drums to half-time) is the terror zenith — the predator at its closest. 300s long, the longest track, because the chase deserves to be lived in (mirrors Telos's 300s apex length exactly — the saga's two peaks are the same duration, opposite emotions). BPM ceiling 118 — do NOT exceed (validation caps at 118) and do NOT let MusicGen push into frantic gabber-EDM; this is a tense relentless DRIVE, the audience is still coding. Outro B (V of E) settles as the chase breaks and primes B minor of track 8 (B as dominant rerooted — the floor drops away again).

tracks.composer_notes: FIRST OF THE BACK-HALF DESCENT TRACKS — the release after the chase. The mode easing from Phrygian-dominant back to natural Aeolian is the harmonic 'breath out' after the apex terror, but unlike Telos's warm settle this is a PLUMMET: the floor drops away and the expedition falls deeper. The BPM begins its descending back-half arc here (the propulsive DESCENDING BPM arc Telos handed off to, now in its falling phase). Keep it driving but less frantic. tracks.composer_notes: The deep turns from hostile to MAJESTIC. The PROPULSION PULSE returns SLOW AND VAST — the same drive ostinato stretched out with enormous reverb, the engine now grand rather than urgent (its track-9 transformation). F# Dorian (raised 6th = D#) lends grandeur, beginning the album's emotional turn from terror toward AWE as it approaches the inner expanse. Magma-glow (deep red-gold) replaces the eerie green of the forests — a warming, deepening palette that foreshadows the inner sun without yet revealing it. Outro C# (V of F#) primes C# minor of track 10 (C# as dominant rerooted — the cooling toward the deepest point).

tracks.composer_notes: The approach to the abyss — cooling, sparse, deepening. C# Aeolian minor and a thinning arrangement (sparse drums, widely-spaced cold bells) signal the air growing thin near the bottom of the descent. The magma glow recedes; the dark deepens toward the album's lowest point (track 11). Keep it sparse and cold but still gently pulsed (not yet drumless — that's track 11). Outro G# (V of C#) primes G#/Ab minor of track 11 (G# as dominant rerooted into the album's deepest, darkest tonic).

tracks.key_relationship_to_prior: Down a perfect fourth from C#m to G#/Ab minor (G# was the V that closed track 10, rerooted as the G#-minor tonic — the descending cycle reaches its DEEPEST AND DARKEST point). Full Phrygian mode (b2 = A) at the album's lowest tonal center — the harmonic floor of the descent, mirroring track 1's threshold but as far below it as the album travels.
tracks.tempo_relationship_to_prior: -6 BPM to 94, the album's near-floor (only the opener's 88 is slower). At the bottom of the world the PROPULSION goes SILENT — this is the album's only other (besides the opener) effectively drumless track, the descending BPM arc reaching its quiet nadir before the handoff. The descent ends not with acceleration but, like the bottom of any fall, with stillness.
tracks.composer_notes: INTERLUDE / DEEPEST DARKEST POINT — the bottom of the world. The shortest track at 210s (mirrors Telos's track-9 interlude length exactly — the saga's two interludes match). The PROPULSION goes SILENT here (no drums) — the engine stops at the bottom of the descent. The DESCENT-CRYSTAL motif returns ALONE AND FALLING: slow, unaccompanied, each phrase dropping into the dark (its track-11 transformation — the crystal heard one last time at the bottom, falling). Full G# Phrygian (b2 = A) is the album's lowest, darkest harmony. CRITICAL: the outro plants a FAINT STIRRING far below — the first hint of something vast and warm waiting beneath even the abyss (the inner-sun promise about to arrive in track 12). Do NOT let MusicGen add a driving kit or a bright lead — this is abyssal stillness and weightless dread. Outro D# (V of G#) primes Eb minor of track 12 (D# = Eb enharmonic; the dominant resolves into the inner-expanse handoff).

tracks.title: Into the Inner Expanse

sections.name: intro
sections.instrumentation: pad shifting from G#/Ab minor into Eb minor as the walls fall away, no drums yet, the DESCENT-CRYSTAL motif turning OUTWARD (the same pitch-set but the contour opening HORIZONTALLY rather than falling, the figure widening as the space opens), the INNER-SUN VOICING returning at last FAINT AND DISTANT on the horizon (low in the mix, heavily filtered, root RISING slowly out of the dark) — withheld for the whole album, here a first rumor

sections.name: verse

tracks.cover_prompt: Lost Worlds saga album cover, cold deep-earth palette opening toward a vast warm inner-sun glow on the far horizon, a lone expedition figure standing at the mouth of a shaft emerging onto a high ledge above an impossibly vast inner-world expanse, the walls of the earth fallen away into open space, a faint warm amber inner sun glowing impossibly far below and ahead across the inner sky, the first warmth after a long cold descent, distant luminous spires hinted on the far horizon (the inner civilization to come), awe emergence threshold mood (handoff to the next chapter), painterly retro-1980s art, no faces visible (figure seen from behind at the brink), no text, 1024x1024
tracks.cover_notes_internal: INNER-EXPANSE HANDOFF cover. The cold deep-earth palette of Hollow Earth gives way in the upper-far frame to the first warm inner-sun glow of the next album (Agartha Vol. 1) — the visual bridge across the saga seam, the inverse of Telos's track-12 handoff cover (warm-above-giving-way-to-cool-below; here cool-foreground-opening-to-warm-distance).
tracks.key_relationship_to_prior: Down a half-step / enharmonic continuation from G#-minor (Ab minor) to Eb minor (D#, the V that closed track 11, resolves into Eb minor — the deepest tonal center opening into the inner-expanse tonic). Eb minor is the album's final home, the bottom of the descent now opening OUTWARD rather than continuing down. The harmony begins, for the first time all album, to lean UPWARD toward warmth.
tracks.tempo_relationship_to_prior: -4 BPM to 90 (mirroring the opener's 88 — the album opens and closes near the same near-floor tempo, the descent's BPM arc rising to the chase apex and falling symmetrically back to a quiet emergence). The propulsion does NOT return; like Telos's handoff, the descent ends in stillness, the next album's job being the arrival.
tracks.composer_notes: CLOSER / SAGA INNER-EXPANSE HANDOFF to AGARTHA, VOL. 1 (album #3). This is the saga's most important structural track after Telos's track 12 — the descent's payoff and the diptych pivot. The abyss does not CLOSE so much as OPEN OUTWARD. THREE things happen as the handoff seed: (1) the DESCENT-CRYSTAL motif turns OUTWARD — the same pitch-set but the contour opens HORIZONTALLY rather than falling, the figure widening as the walls fall away (its track-12 transformation, handing the motif toward Agartha's radiant return); (2) the INNER-SUN VOICING returns AT LAST — withheld for the entire album, it surfaces here FAINT AND DISTANT with its root RISING out of the dark, the first promise of the inner sun the expedition has descended toward (it does NOT bloom — that is Agartha's job); (3) the closing cadence settles onto an OPEN Bb major-minor DOMINANT that leans UPWARD AND OUTWARD toward the distant light and is LEFT OPEN — NOT a resolved home cadence but a threshold that opens into the inner world. SAGA HANDOFF: a listener moving from this final open dominant into Agartha Vol. 1 track 1 should feel the deep give way to the inner sun, the saga turning from descent toward arrival — Agartha should OPEN near this distant inner-sun statement and RESOLVE INTO it at full bloom as the saga's payoff. This inverts Telos's track-12 handoff exactly: Telos left the inner sun receding UPWARD into a darkening descent; Hollow Earth leaves it rising DISTANT into a warming emergence. Do NOT let MusicGen resolve this to a closed home chord or add a driving kit — the OPEN, UPWARD-LEANING, AWED ending IS the composition and the saga seam. 270s long to give the emergence its full weight (mirrors Telos's track-12 270s length — the saga's two handoffs match).

album_notes.overall_form: Threshold -> Descent -> Megafauna Apex -> Long Fall -> Inner-Expanse Handoff over 12 tracks, a DOWNWARD-DARKENING-then-AWED arc that inverts Telos's upward-warming one. This is the saga's descent album and the inverse twin of Telos: where Telos warmed minor toward Lydian light over a low meditative BPM band, Hollow Earth drives minor DOWN through ever-darker modes over a rising-then-falling propulsive BPM arc. The shape is a tension-arch, not a symmetric ABA: THRESHOLD (tracks 1-3): the polar opening Telos's floor became, drumless and cold (1, Bb minor), the propulsion igniting as the dive begins (2, F Dorian), the first vast hollow gallery (3, C Dorian). DEEPENING (tracks 4-7): the bioluminescent forests where life first stirs (4, G minor, Phrygian color beginning), the black underground sea with megafauna shadows (5, D minor Phrygian), the bone-floored predator ground (6, A minor Phrygian), and the MEGAFAUNA APEX (7, E minor Phrygian-dominant) — the album's peak BPM (118) and most menacing harmony, the great pursuit. LONG FALL (tracks 8-11): the chase breaks and the floor drops away (8, B minor), the magma-lit galleries the size of cities where terror turns to grandeur (9, F# Dorian), the air thinning toward the bottom (10, C# minor), and the abyssal gulf at the bottom of the world where the propulsion goes silent and the descent-crystal falls alone (11, G#/Ab Phrygian, the deepest darkest point). INNER-EXPANSE HANDOFF (track 12, Eb minor): the abyss opens outward into a vast inner space, the inner-sun voicing returns at last faint and distant, and the cadence lands on an open upward-leaning dominant that hands off to Agartha, Vol. 1. The cold threshold of track 1's Bb minor and the awed emergence of track 12's Eb minor frame the descent; the inner sun, present in Telos and withheld through all of Hollow Earth, returns in the final bar as a distant promise — that withholding-and-return is the album's thesis made structural and the diptych pivot toward Agartha.
album_notes.key_strategy: A DESCENDING-CYCLE progression that mirrors and inverts Telos's upward-warming one — the saga's descent-to-deep arc. Where Telos warmed minor toward Lydian light, Hollow Earth drives minor DOWN through a descending cycle of perfect fourths/fifths, the MODE DARKENING progressively (Dorian -> Aeolian -> Phrygian -> Phrygian-dominant) as the descent deepens, then easing back toward Aeolian as terror turns to awe near the bottom. The progression: Bbm Aeolian (threshold, resolving Telos's open F dominant DOWN into the dark) -> Fm Dorian (the dominant Telos left, rerooted as propulsion) -> Cm Dorian (down a 4th) -> Gm Aeolian (down a 4th, mode darkens from Dorian warmth to natural minor as life stirs) -> Dm Phrygian-flavor (down a 4th, the b2 creeps in; note Dm is Telos's home tonic, here re-cast dark) -> Am Phrygian (down a 4th, full Phrygian darkness) -> Em Phrygian-dominant (down a 4th, the chromatic-intensified APEX, darkest/most exotic harmony) -> Bm Aeolian (down a 4th, mode eases as the chase breaks) -> F#m Dorian (down a 4th, Dorian grandeur replaces menace) -> C#m Aeolian (down a 4th, cooling toward the bottom) -> G#/Abm Phrygian (down a 4th, the DEEPEST darkest tonal center and harmony) -> Ebm Aeolian (the inner-expanse handoff, opening outward, leaning UP toward warmth for the first time). The descent runs an almost-complete cycle of fourths down (Bb-F-C-G-D-A-E-B-F#-C#-G#-Eb), the harmonic engine of the literal downward journey. The closing cadence is an OPEN Bb major-minor dominant leaning UPWARD AND OUTWARD toward the inner sun — the saga seam to Agartha, the exact INVERSE of Telos's open-dominant-leaning-DOWN handoff. NO prior album runs a full descending cycle of fourths with progressive modal darkening; Hollow Earth is the saga's purpose-built descent.
album_notes.tempo_strategy: The PROPULSIVE DESCENDING-then-RISING-then-DESCENDING arc the saga's descent album is built around — the literal opposite of Telos's low meditative band. Hollow Earth's BPM RISES through the descent's first half (the dive accelerating) to the megafauna apex, then FALLS symmetrically through the long fall to a quiet emergence: THRESHOLD 88 (drumless, the held breath; one step up from Telos's 82 handoff) -> 96 (propulsion ignites, the album's first big +8 jump) -> 102. DEEPENING 108 -> 112 -> 116 -> 118 (the megafauna apex, the BPM CEILING at 118 — the validation max, deliberately maxed for the chase). LONG FALL 112 -> 106 -> 100 -> 94 (the abyssal nadir; the descending back-half arc, the propulsion thinning toward the bottom). HANDOFF 90 (the inner-expanse emergence, near the opener's 88 — the album frames its descent between two quiet near-floor tempos, the engine rising to the chase and falling back to awe). The band 88-118 is the WIDEST and HIGHEST of the saga so far (Telos was 80-104, the lowest/narrowest) — Hollow Earth is the saga's driving record, built to MOVE where Telos was built to meditate, even though both serve the same 2am-coding audience (118 is a relentless DRIVE, not a rave — never frantic, no gabber). All BPMs within the 80-118 validation range (album range 88-118; 118 is exactly the validation ceiling — do not exceed).
album_notes.motif_strategy: Three motifs: TWO saga-recurring leitmotifs (carried forward from Telos and transformed for the descent) and one album-local figure. (1) DESCENT-CRYSTAL (saga leitmotif #1, transposed-and-darkened from Telos's CRYSTAL ARPEGGIO) — Telos's bright ascending crystal-city arpeggio, here transposed into Hollow Earth's low register and DARKENED to a minor-add9 shape with its CONTOUR FALLING. Introduced ringing the threshold in track 1 (picking up exactly where Telos track 12 fragmented-and-turned-it-downward — the saga seam made audible); FRAGMENTED AND DRIVEN into a propulsive 16th-note ostinato under the predator ground in track 6 (the crystal weaponised into rhythm); CHROMATICALLY DESCENDING through the Phrygian-dominant chase apex in track 7 (the falling contour stretched into a long plunge); ALONE AND FALLING, slow and unaccompanied over the abyssal pad in track 11 (the crystal heard at the bottom of the world); and turned OUTWARD (the contour opening horizontally as the walls fall away) at the inner-expanse handoff in track 12, handing the motif toward Agartha. SAGA NOTE: Agartha Vol. 1 (album #3) should return this figure at its most RADIANT near the inner sun, transposed up into bright register — the payoff inversion of this darkened descent statement. (2) INNER-SUN VOICING (saga leitmotif #2, WITHHELD) — the wide warm major chord Telos bloomed at its apex, here DELIBERATELY WITHHELD for the entire album: heard NOWHERE in tracks 1-11 (its absence IS the descent — the deep is cold and lightless, the warmth left above), returning ONLY in track 12 FAINT AND DISTANT on the horizon with its root RISING out of the dark, the first rumor of the inner sun. Hollow Earth's job is to make the listener WANT it back by withholding it; Agartha's job is to give it at full bloom (Agartha should open near and resolve INTO this voicing). (3) PROPULSION PULSE (album-local drive figure) — a driving low bass-and-kick eighth-note ostinato, the engine of the descent and what makes Hollow Earth MOVE where Telos meditated. Introduced as the dive ignites in track 2; syncopated octave-jump in track 4; harder and faster under the predator ground in track 6; most relentless at the chase apex in track 7; and returning SLOW AND VAST with enormous reverb through the magma galleries in track 9 (grand rather than urgent). Deliberately ABSENT from the slow/quiet tracks (1, 11, 12) where the dark and the awe take over.
album_notes.tracklist_continuity: Each track ends on a pitch that primes the next track's tonic — almost entirely via the descending cycle of fourths (each track closes on its own dominant, which is rerooted as the next track's tonic, the harmonic engine of the literal downward journey). Specifically: 1 ends leaning toward F (V of Bb, unresolved — the dive about to begin; F rerooted as track 2's F-minor tonic). 2 ends on C (V of F, rerooted as track 3's C minor). 3 ends on G (V of C, rerooted as track 4's G minor). 4 ends on D (V of G, rerooted as track 5's D minor). 5 ends on A (V of D, rerooted as track 6's A minor). 6 ends on E (V of A, reread as the dominant intensifying into track 7's E Phrygian-dominant chase apex). 7 ends on B (V of E, the chase breaking, rerooted as track 8's B minor). 8 ends on F# (V of B, rerooted as track 9's F# minor). 9 ends on C# (V of F#, rerooted as track 10's C# minor). 10 ends on G# (V of C#, rerooted as track 11's G#/Ab minor). 11 ends on D# (V of G#; D# = Eb enharmonic, resolving into track 12's Eb minor). 12 ends on an OPEN Bb major-minor DOMINANT leaning UPWARD AND OUTWARD toward the inner sun — the saga seam, NOT a closed cadence; it hands off to Agartha Vol. 1 track 1. The listener won't consciously hear the descending cycle — they will feel the relentless downward pull as inevitable, the megafauna apex as the bottom of a real fall, and the final open dominant as the deep giving way to a distant light.
album_notes.saga_handoff_notes: This is album #2 of the 5-album Lost Worlds shared universe (Telos -> Hollow Earth -> Agartha -> Atlantis -> Gate of Ra) and the FIRST HALF of the Hollow-Earth/Agartha DIPTYCH. Hollow Earth OPENS from the seam Telos left: Telos track 12 closed on an open F major-minor dominant leaning DOWN into Bb minor with the crystal motif fragmented-and-falling and the inner-sun voicing receding upward; Hollow Earth track 1 opens in Bb minor, picks up the falling crystal contour (now the darkened low-register DESCENT-CRYSTAL), and confirms the inner-sun's absence (it stays withheld for eleven tracks). The album CARRIES FORWARD both saga leitmotifs: the CRYSTAL ARPEGGIO (transposed-and-darkened into the DESCENT-CRYSTAL — Telos's bright ascending crystal city motif heard from far below, low-register minor-add9, contour falling) and the INNER-SUN VOICING (withheld through the whole descent so its single distant return in track 12 lands as the saga's first promise of arrival). The CROSS-ALBUM HANDOFF lives in track 12 ('Into the Inner Expanse'): the abyss opens OUTWARD into a vast inner space, the DESCENT-CRYSTAL turns its contour OUTWARD (handing toward Agartha), the INNER-SUN VOICING returns FAINT AND DISTANT with its root RISING (the first rumor of the inner sun), and the cadence lands on an OPEN Bb major-minor dominant leaning UPWARD AND OUTWARD toward that light — the exact INVERSE of Telos's open-dominant-leaning-DOWN handoff. The next album, Agartha Vol. 1 (the diptych payoff / saga inner-sun arrival), should OPEN near this distant inner-sun statement and RESOLVE INTO it at full bloom, returning the crystal motif at its most radiant near the inner sun. A listener playing the saga end-to-end should feel Telos as the warm launch keystone whose floor opens, Hollow Earth as the cold propulsive DESCENT that withholds the light, and the seam from Hollow Earth's final bar as the deep giving way to the inner sun — the saga turning from descent toward arrival.
album_notes.visual_continuity_notes: Twelve covers narrate the threshold -> dive -> deepening -> megafauna apex -> long fall -> inner-expanse-emergence journey within the shared Lost Worlds saga visual family, but with the protagonist and palette evolved for the descent: the LONE FIGURE persists (always seen from behind, no faces) but is now an EXPEDITION figure (fur-and-canvas polar/Vernian gear, not Telos's white robe — the saga's protagonist as explorer rather than survivor); the THRESHOLD/PORTAL persists (the polar opening in track 1, the inner-expanse mouth in track 12); the GLOW-FROM-BELOW persists but INVERTED — in Telos it was the warm inner sun seen from above; here it is withheld and cold until the final cover's distant promise. The Hollow-Earth-specific palette is COLD DEEP-EARTH: indigo, teal, ice-blue, aurora-green, near-black, with magma deep-red-gold appearing only in the deepest galleries (track 9) and the first warm amber inner-sun glow returning only on the far horizon of the final cover (track 12) — the deliberate inverse of Telos's warm-alpine palette. Track 1: figure at the rim of the polar opening, aurora vortex overhead (cold threshold). Track 2: the spiralling ice shaft, plunging. Track 3: the first vast hollow gallery opening. Track 4: the bioluminescent fungal forests (eerie green-violet). Track 5: the black underground sea, megafauna shadow beneath (ominous). Track 6: the bone-floored hunting ground, waking predator (menacing). Track 7: the great pursuit through stone trees — the album's HERO cover, most dramatic. Track 8: the floor dropping away into a black shaft (plummet). Track 9: the magma-lit city-sized galleries (grand, the palette's only deep warmth). Track 10: the narrowing cold descent, air thinning. Track 11: the abyssal gulf, near-total black with one distant warm point (the bottom). Track 12: the inner-expanse mouth, walls fallen away, the faint warm inner sun on the far horizon with distant spires hinted (the visual saga-seam handoff to Agartha Vol. 1 — cool-foreground-opening-to-warm-distance, the inverse of Telos's warm-above-giving-way-to-cool-below). Every cover_prompt ends with 'no text, 1024x1024' and keeps faces hidden. The covers should read as one cold propulsive descent and as the second chapter of the five-album saga.
album_notes.execution_notes_for_pipeline: MusicGen / ACE-Step is the renderer of record (license risk accepted). Every musicgen_prompt is explicit about 'instrumental' and 'no vocals'; all choir references are phrased as 'wordless choir pad' (textural, no lyrics, no phonemes) — do not relax this in any per-track override (the prior synthwave albums never sang; the saga's choir is pad texture only). Per-track durations sum to 2970 seconds (49.5 minutes) for the 12 — comfortably over the 45-minute album floor. Long tracks: 1 (270s, the threshold needs room), 7 (300s, the megafauna apex, longest — matches Telos's 300s apex), 12 (270s, the inner-expanse handoff — matches Telos's 270s handoff). Interlude track 11 is the shortest at 210s (matches Telos's track-9 interlude length). The rest sit at 240s. All BPMs within the 80-118 validation range (album range 88-118; track 7 sits at exactly the 118 ceiling — do NOT exceed). WEATHER ROUTING — ACTION REQUIRED, FLAG FOR HUMAN/PIPELINE: the theme's weather anchor is ARCTIC (polar entrance), but `nightdrive-encoder::weather::region_for` (crates/nightdrive-encoder/src/weather.rs ~line 263) routes ARCTIC ONLY on the slug substrings 'arctic' / 'ice-station' / 'ice_station'. The album_slug 'hollow-earth-vol-1' contains NONE of these, so as-shipped it would FALL THROUGH to a hashed US region (wrong). BEFORE ENCODING, add a routing keyword: in region_for, add `if id.contains("hollow") || id.contains("polar") { return &ARCTIC; }` (mirroring how 'telos'/'shasta' were both added for the Lost Worlds launch). The ARCTIC region (Reykjavik/Nuuk/Murmansk/Yellowknife, Open-Meteo forecast + RainViewer radar; RainViewer covers only Reykjavik, others render basemap-only per the weather.rs note) is otherwise ready. Cover prompts are SDXL-ready (1024x1024, faces hidden, lone-figure-from-behind to avoid realistic-face generation); per the future-albums native-aspect-gen rule, generate covers at native aspect buckets (1024 square + 1344x768 + 768x1344) from scratch rather than outpainting. Per the sync-drop rule for future albums, target a 1-shot synchronized public flip at one anchor timestamp (use run-album --publish-at), NOT the per-track trickle. Mind the YouTube caps for a 12-track album: the ~10/day channel rolling-24h cap, the 6/day GCP per-project video.insert cap, and Pacific-midnight resets — span the upload across a reset or stagger (publish-staggered 6/day) and set the sync-drop anchor accordingly, per the Vol. 2/Vol. 3 lessons.
album_notes.differentiation_from_prior_albums: Hollow Earth is the saga's DESCENT album and the deliberate INVERSE TWIN of Telos. Where Telos was the catalog's first WARM, elegy-to-transcendence record (minor warming to Lydian light, lowest/narrowest BPM band 80-104, warm alpine palette, meditative), Hollow Earth is COLD, PROPULSIVE, and DARKENING: minor driving DOWN through a descending cycle of fourths with progressive modal darkening (Dorian -> Aeolian -> Phrygian -> Phrygian-dominant), the saga's WIDEST and HIGHEST BPM band (88-118, peaking at the 118 validation ceiling for the megafauna chase) versus Telos's meditative 80-104, and a cold deep-earth palette (indigo/teal/aurora-green/near-black with magma red-gold and a single distant inner-sun promise) versus Telos's warm alpine peach-rose-gold. It shares the saga's two recurring leitmotifs but transforms them as inverses: Telos's bright ASCENDING crystal arpeggio becomes Hollow Earth's darkened FALLING low-register descent-crystal, and Telos's BLOOMING inner-sun voicing is WITHHELD for the whole album (its absence is the descent) until a single distant rising return in the final bar. Versus the other catalog albums: it shares Neo-Tokyo's propulsive vertical-descent energy and Phrygian darkening but pushes further into Phrygian-dominant menace and adds the saga's recurring cross-album material; it is darker and faster than Sunset/Tron and, unlike all of them, is purpose-built as the middle of a five-album narrative — it neither opens cleanly (it picks up Telos's open dominant) nor closes cleanly (it hands off to Agartha on an open upward-leaning dominant). Mike Oldfield + Brian Eno's vastness crossed with Com Truise / Kavinsky's propulsion and Tangerine Dream's dark sequencer drive — the reverent warmth of Telos turned cold and set MOVING into the deep.
